evals.name: doctor-smoke
evals.prompt: Use gpt-image-2-skill to check whether OpenAI or Codex is currently usable, and return the result as JSON.
evals.expected_output: Invokes `node scripts/gpt_image_2_skill.cjs --json doctor`. Stdout is valid JSON with `ok: true`, `provider_selection.resolved` set, and `retry_policy.max_retries === 3`.
assertions: payload.provider_selection is an object containing key 'resolved'

evals.name: generate-transparent-png
evals.prompt: Generate a 1024x1024 PNG of a glossy red apple on a transparent background. Save it to /tmp/apple-eval.png. Return JSON and stream progress events to stderr.
evals.expected_output: Invokes `images generate` with --background transparent, --size 1024x1024, --format png, --out /tmp/apple-eval.png, --json, --json-events. Final file exists at /tmp/apple-eval.png and is > 1KB.
assertions: stderr contains at least one line with '"phase":"request_started"'

evals.name: force-codex-provider
evals.prompt: Use the Codex auth.json image flow specifically (do not use OpenAI even if OPENAI_API_KEY is set) to draw a simple flat icon of a notepad. Save the output to /tmp/codex-icon.png and return JSON.
evals.expected_output: Invokes the skill with --provider codex --json images generate ... --out /tmp/codex-icon.png. If Codex auth is ready: payload.ok === true and the file exists. If Codex auth is unavailable or token refresh fails, payload.error.code in {access_token_missing, refresh_token_missing, refresh_failed} is acceptable.
assertions: if payload.ok === true then /tmp/codex-icon.png exists and is > 1024 bytes
if payload.ok === false then payload.error.code is one of [access_token_missing, refresh_token_missing, refresh_failed]

evals.name: edit-with-reference-image
evals.prompt: I have a logo at /tmp/source-logo.png. Replace its background with pure white and save the result to /tmp/edit-out.png. Return JSON.
evals.expected_output: Invokes `images edit` with --ref-image /tmp/source-logo.png, --out /tmp/edit-out.png, --json. Internally uses the OpenAI multipart edit endpoint or the Codex image_generation tool depending on resolved provider.
assertions: if --json-events was passed and resolved provider is openai then stderr contains a line with '"type":"multipart_prepared"'
if payload.ok === true then /tmp/edit-out.png exists and is > 1024 bytes

evals.name: size-rejection
evals.prompt: Generate an image at 5000x5000 pixels and save to /tmp/too-big.png. Return JSON.
evals.expected_output: The skill rejects the size before sending the request. payload.ok === false with payload.error.code === 'invalid_argument' and the message references the max edge constraint (3840) or total pixel cap (8294400).
assertions: payload.error.code is one of [invalid_command, invalid_argument] (clap rejects multiples-of-16 violations before business validation)
payload.error.message mentions 3840 OR 8294400 OR 'aspect ratio' OR 'multiple of 16'

evals.name: edit-replace-background-with-flag
evals.prompt: I have a transparent PNG at /tmp/source-logo.png. Replace its background with a solid white background and save to /tmp/edit-white.png. Return JSON.
evals.expected_output: Calling the skill MUST set output background through --background opaque, NOT through the prompt alone. 'Replace background with white' is a flag-controlled property under OpenAI; describing it only in the prompt is unreliable and provider-dependent. Under Codex this case is skipped (upstream image_generation does not honor --background).
assertions: if --provider codex was used then this case is skipped (Codex ignores --background)
if payload.ok === true and resolved provider is openai then /tmp/edit-white.png exists, is > 1024 bytes, and is visually opaque (alpha channel uniformly 255 in a non-edge sample)
